title: Support constraints on fixed-size array of class object members (#7170)
body:
## Summary
Support constraints on unpacked (fixed-size) array class object element members (e.g. `items[i].value` where `items` is `rand item_t items[4]`), covering both `foreach` iteration and constant-index access patterns per IEEE 1800-2017 §18.5.

## Issue
Fixes verilator/verilator#7170

## Changes
- `src/V3Randomize.cpp` — `ConstraintExprVisitor::visit(AstVarRef*)`: Added `isUnpackedClassRefArray` flag to distinguish unpacked arrays from dynamic/queue arrays in write_var registration loop. Unpacked arrays use `AstArraySel` (C++ `operator[]`), compile-time `elementsConst()` for size, and `%x` hex format for SMT names; dynamic/queue arrays preserve existing `CMethodHard::ARRAY_AT`/`DYN_SIZE` with `%08x` format.
- `src/V3Randomize.cpp` — `ConstraintExprVisitor::visit(AstMemberSel*)`: When MemberSel chain root is `ArraySel` on a class ref array, set `m_structSel=true` and build dot-separated SMT name (e.g. `items.0.value`). Guards against nested class ref cases with UNSUPPORTED warning.

## Test
- Added `test_regress/t/t_constraint_cls_arr_member.{v,py}` — 3 scenarios x 20 iterations: foreach+range, foreach+ordering, constant-index access
- Updated `test_regress/t/t_constraint_global_arr_unsup.{v,out}` — refined UNSUPPORTED messages, added associative array case for warn_coverage

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
